review_execution.command: claude -p --model claude-opus-4-8 --effort max --permission-mode dontAsk --tools "" --output-format json < staged diff prompt > /tmp/cp00-120-claude-review-output-valid.json

findings.title: Deny reasons and search-trim outcomes depend on evaluate.js semantics outside this diff
findings.evidence: legal_hold_interaction/ethical_wall_interaction assert decision.reason equals the rule's custom reason; object_acl_interaction expects reason 'object_acl_deny'; search_decision_binding/security_trimming_proof expect trimSearchResults() to drop the cross-tenant (t_other) and ACL-denied (d_cp120_acl_denied) entries, leaving only d_cp120_document. These outcomes are produced by evaluatePermission/trimSearchResults in ./evaluate.js, which is reused unchanged and is not part of this diff.
findings.recommendation: No action required. The expectations are self-consistent and double-enforced by the new node:test cases and scripts/validate-rp02-permission-kernel-contract.mjs, and evaluate.js is the same evaluator already exercised by closed packs CP110-CP119. Flagged only to make the cross-file reliance explicit.

parsed_review.rationale: The pack matches the planned Risk C scope. Unit math is exact and self-consistent: CP120_FIXTURE_PHASES (M06 S06-S20=15, M07=22, M08=20, M09=20, M10=8) total 85, and CP120_PERMISSION_MATRIX_PHASES (M00=11, M01=20, M02=20, M03=14) total 65, summing to 150 across 9 micro-phases with first unit RP02.P05.M06.S06 (continuing CP119's RP02.P05.M06.S05) and last unit RP02.P06.M03.S14, handing off to CP00-121/RP02.P06.M03.S15. Deliverable counts independently recompute to the validator's hardcoded targets (fixture 29, claude_review 8, implementation 59, security_audit 22, test 16, hermes_evidence 4, ui 12) and area counts (85/11/20/20/14), so the coverage validator and tests are satisfiable. CP119 inheritance is wired via createPermissionKernelCp119FixtureWorkflowMatrix and the CP119 contract id (inherited_workflow_case_count 9, base_fixture_binding_count 4). All 19 decision bindings route safely: view -> completed_metadata_only, search/security-trimming returns only the authorized id with omitted_result_policy 'do_not_count_or_render_unauthorized_results' and unauthorized_count_exposed=false, mutation/share -> approval_required, export/download/review -> review_required, AI retrieval -> blocked_ai_retrieval_boundary, deny-over-allow -> deny, with audit_hint and audit_event_expectation preview-only (emitted_to_audit_ledger=false) and permission_fixture persisted=false. The file performs no I/O, network, or DB calls; evaluatePermission/trimSearchResults are pure; every result passes through freezeNoWriteResult, and the 16-flag no-write attestation (no runtime UI/API route, policy mutation, audit/product/database write, idempotency/lock persistence, rollback/retry, external share/export, AI/analytics execution) plus ldip_implemented=false and hrx_embedded_boundary are asserted in source, tests, and the JSON contract. Contract public_exports, decision_binding_ids, and hidden_source_fields match the actual module exports and are cross-checked by the validator; listing hidden field names is a redaction spec, not a value leak (hidden_field_names_exposed=false). No P0-P2 issues found.
